articles.description: Pipe, a general-purpose visual programming language addressing AI-driven software risks, scored 86 on HackerNoon's Proof of Usefulness algorithm, landing in the lowest positive tier. The gap is exactly where expected: no live product yet means no traction or adoption evidence. Here's what's actually being built to close it.

articles.title: The 8 Best Contract Management Software Tools Worth Using in 2026
articles.description: Modern contract management software has moved beyond simple digital storage, with AI-powered platforms now actively analyzing agreements to optimize spend and flag financial risks. The right choice depends on your team's specific needs, ranging from streamlined sales document automation to comprehensive tools that integrate vendor and contract data.

articles.title: Testing AI Agents and Testing With AI Agents Are Two Sides of the Same Coin
articles.description: Modern QA has two sides. Side One: AI agents now automate test generation, self-heal broken scripts, and synthesize test data — cutting execution time by up to 30%. Side Two: The AI agents your team ships are probabilistic, non-deterministic, and cannot be validated with traditional test scripts. A 3-step agent chain with 70% per-step accuracy fails 66% of the time end-to-end. This article breaks down how to build validation pipelines for both and why treating them as one problem is the most common mistake engineering teams make.
